task.title: Phase 3E Exercise: DITA-OT Architecture Exploration
task.shortdesc: Trace the DITA-OT preprocessing pipeline, inspect temporary files, and map extension points. Primary track for software engineers.

ul.li: Completed Phase 2 exercises (TaskFlow docs with map and reuse)
Read Part 7 topics: DITA-OT Developer Handbook, OT Mental Model, Preprocessing Deep Dive
Familiarity with XML and command-line tools

context.p: Before building plugins, you need to understand what DITA-OT does internally. This exercise has you run builds with debug flags and inspect the intermediate files that DITA-OT produces — the "temp" directory is where the magic happens.

step.cmd: Run a debug build to see the full processing log.

info.codeblock: dita -i taskflow-docs/taskflow.ditamap -f html5 \ -o out/taskflow-debug \ --debug \ --temp=temp/taskflow-debug 2>&1 | tee build-debug.log

ul.li: --debug — maximum verbosity in the log
--temp — preserves the temp directory (normally cleaned up)

stepresult.p: The temp directory is preserved at temp/taskflow-debug/ and the full log is saved to build-debug.log.

step.cmd: Explore the temp directory to understand preprocessing stages.

info.codeblock:
# List the temp directory structure find temp/taskflow-debug -type f | head -30
# Look at the key files ls -la temp/taskflow-debug/

ul.li: *.dita files — preprocessed versions of your topics (conrefs resolved, keys expanded)
job.xml — the build configuration DITA-OT generated
Any .list files — track which files go where

step.cmd: Compare your original topic with its preprocessed version in temp.

info.codeblock: # Compare the original and preprocessed versions diff taskflow-docs/installing-taskflow.dita \ temp/taskflow-debug/installing-taskflow.dita

ul.li: Conref elements replaced with actual content
Keyref elements resolved to their values
Added xtrf and xtrc tracing attributes
Class attributes normalized

info.note: This is how DITA-OT separates concerns: preprocessing handles content assembly (conref, keyref, filtering), then the transform only deals with presentation. Understanding this split is essential for plugin development.

step.cmd: Trace preprocessing stages in the debug log.

info.codeblock:
# Find preprocessing stage markers grep -n "preprocess\|keyref\|conref\|maplink\|chunk" build-debug.log | head -20
# Find the transform stage grep -n "transform\|xslt\|html5" build-debug.log | head -20
info.p: Document what you find: List the preprocessing stages in order. Typical stages include: gen-list, debug-filter, mapref, keyref, conref, maplink, move-meta, chunk.

step.cmd: List all available extension points for the HTML5 transform.

info.codeblock:
# List installed plugins dita plugins
# Check what extension points exist # Look at the HTML5 plugin's plugin.xml find "$(dita --install-dir 2>/dev/null || echo $DITA_HOME)" \ -path "*/org.dita.html5/plugin.xml" -exec cat {} \;

ul.li: dita.xsl.html5 — XSLT stylesheet override
dita.conductor.html5.param — parameter injection
dita.xsl.html5.cover — cover page override
dita.xsl.html5.toc — TOC override

info.note: Extension points are the only safe way to customize DITA-OT output. Direct modification of built-in plugin files will break on upgrade.

step.cmd: Read the main HTML5 XSLT stylesheet to understand the transform architecture.

info.p: Note how it uses <xsl:import> to compose multiple stylesheets. Your plugin overrides will use the same import mechanism.

step.cmd: Draw the DITA-OT processing pipeline from your observations.

info.p: Based on what you learned, sketch or write out the pipeline:

ol.li: Transform: XSLT stylesheets convert to target format

info.p: Compare your diagram with the architecture diagram in the training materials.

p.b: Self-Check — you should now be able to:

ul.li: ✓ Run a debug build and preserve temp files
✓ Explain what preprocessing does (conref, keyref, filtering resolution)
✓ Find and read preprocessed files in the temp directory
✓ Identify the key extension points for customization
✓ Trace the full pipeline from input map to output HTML
✓ Explain why extension points are the safe customization path
